description: Lock Merchant Accounting Ledgers to source-backed Renaissance Venetian multi-book merchant accounting rather than Lloyd's insurance-office bookkeeping.

checks.reason: The corrected node is anchored to Andrea Barbarigo's 1430 Venetian journal and ledger case.
The scoped ledger system relies on diffused Italian double-entry methods and coordinated journal/ledger accounts.
Bills of exchange supplied long-distance credit activity recorded by merchant ledgers but were not physical ledger components.
Lloyd's insurance-office brokerage is later and outside the corrected Venetian merchant-ledger scope.
